name: Cream of Shiitake Mushroom Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 quart Milk, whole, low-fat or skim
1 small Onion, peeled and stuck with 2 Cloves
4 tbsp Rice flour (or barley or oat flour)
recipeInstructions:
This rich, earthy soup has but 100 calories per serving if made with non-fat milk.
1. PLACE THE MILK, ONION, peppercorns, salt and bouquet garni in a saucepan and bring slowly to a boil.
2. FORM A smooth paste of the rice flour and the cold milk.
3. ADD the paste into the just-boiling milk and stir briskly until there are no lumps.
4. SIMMER over very low heat for 20 minutes.
5. Meanwhile, CUT OFF the tough ends of the mushroom stems. RESERVE a few mushroom slices for garnish and CHOP the remaining mushrooms coarsely.
6. ADD the chopped mushrooms to the saucepan and CONTINUE SIMMING 5 minutes.
7. FINALLY, ADD the cream and Madeira, if desired, and SERVE in hot bowls, garnished with a slice of mushroom.
